message:
refactor(dashboard): deduplicate WindowSizeMsg handler, add reduced-motion tick rates

The Update() method previously had a full inline copy of the
handleWindowSize() logic (~50 lines) that duplicated the pointer-
receiver method defined earlier in the file. This caused the two code
paths to drift: handleWindowSize() included ensembleModes.SetSize()
sizing, while the Update() copy did not, and vice versa for debug
logging guards.

Changes:
- Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg) now delegates entirely to handleWindowSize(),
  eliminating the duplicated block. The return value is propagated
  correctly (handleWindowSize already returns (tea.Model, tea.Cmd)).
- handleWindowSize() gains ensembleModes.SetSize() call with min 30x10
  clamping, which was previously only in the Update() copy.
- Render cache invalidation narrowed: only clears renderedOutputCache
  when width changes (not height-only resizes), since rendered panel
  strings are width-dependent but height changes only affect viewport
  scrolling.
- Tier transition log.Printf is now gated behind dashboardDebugEnabled()
  to avoid unconditional logging in production.
- handleWindowSize() return type changed from (tea.Model, tea.Cmd)
  returning *m to returning the value *m directly, consistent with
  Bubble Tea model patterns.

Reduced motion integration:
- New() now initializes reduceMotion from styles.ReducedMotionEnabled(),
  which auto-enables inside tmux/screen/CI.
- When reduceMotion is true, baseTick slows from 100ms to 250ms and
  idleTick from 500ms to 1s, reducing unnecessary repaints in
  multiplexed terminals where each tick causes visible flicker.

Tests:
- TestNew_ReducedMotionAutoDisablesInTmux: verifies reduceMotion=true
  and 250ms base tick inside tmux.
- TestNew_ReducedMotionCanBeForcedOff: verifies NTM_ANIMATIONS=1
  overrides tmux detection, keeping 100ms base tick.
- TestHandleWindowSizeOnlyClearsRenderCacheOnWidthChange: verifies
  height-only resize preserves cache while width change clears it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
